splits.definition: Reference past survival to sustain through new challenges.

members: Framing Life as Happening For You
splits.definition: Construct a larger story about hardship to build identity and resilience.
Hard paths act as selection filters; difficulty proves few follow.

definition: Reframe hardship via narrative, reference, or competitive lens.
rationale: Cluster conflates three distinct mechanisms: anchoring to past, narrative structure, and competitive advantage. Splits separate causation models.

kick_members.headline: Currently doing almost nothing to acquire agents despite 4 open desks
kick_members.reason: Context-dependent anecdote; fails resolvable referents (A1) without company/source binding. Vague actor reference ('you').

definition: Sustained deal flow volume as market penetration metric.
rationale: Deal Flow is load-bearing; Agent Recruitment headline is too circumstantial and references undefined entity.

definition: Document post-certification earnings lift as credible value signal.
rationale: Two paraphrases of same claim; high similarity (0.96); centroid is clean. Accept as-is.

kick_members.headline: Critical differentiator for business value; determines whether growth compounds or requires constant new customer acquisition
kick_members.reason: Duplicate of prior headline with marginal wording change. Keep line 138; drop line 144.

definition: Retention >80% annual enables compounding growth; churn <20% is critical threshold.
rationale: Tight clustering (0.91 min sim) on coherent theme. Remove duplicate line 144 before finalization.

definition: Feast-famine cycles reflect inconsistent culture favoring mercenary over mission alignment.
rationale: Current name is passive; 'volatility driver' makes the causal claim active and load-bearing.

splits.definition: Dominate one lead source fully before adding others; concentration enables negotiating power.
Single strong brand with clear winner outperforms portfolio of 4-5 juggled products.

definition: Focus beats diversification in both channels and products.
rationale: Channel selection (inbound vs. outbound, repeatable vs. scattered) is distinct from product portfolio strategy (brand equity vs. SKU dilution).

definition: First-mover community advantage creates permanent market lock-in via network density.
rationale: Current name is passive; 'network effects & moat' surfaces the economic mechanism.

definition: Target 27% net margins as profitability floor, benchmarked against Panda Express corporate efficiency.
rationale: Two paraphrases; one has 3 source citations, indicating load-bearing claim. High similarity (0.98); accept with unified definition.

definition: Data architecture must precede AI layer; AI amplifies existing data, not creates structure from nothing.
rationale: Near-duplicate paraphrases (0.98 similarity). Accept as single claim; remove redundant member.

splits.definition: RPM (revenue per 1k views) and behavioral influence outperform vanity metrics like raw views.
Ad revenue is quality metric, not primary income; books, courses, affiliate drive real dollars.

definition: Measure business impact via revenue efficiency, not engagement vanity metrics.
rationale: Conflates two ideas: (1) what metric to use for a single revenue stream (RPM), and (2) which revenue streams to prioritize (diversification). Split separates measurement from strategy.

kick_members.headline: The concrete business has $5M top line with 50% gross margin, but 10% net margin and is split across two businesses, making it impossible to exit or hire an operator.
kick_members.reason: Fails A1 (ambiguous actor reference—'the concrete business', 'it')—undefined entity. Too case-specific; not generalizable without company name and context.

definition: 1-3M revenue range traps founder between overwork and betting 50% profit on single hire.
rationale: Swamp pattern is load-bearing; Two-Business headline is anecdotal and context-dependent. Kick it; keep cluster.

canonical_name: Licensing Model vs. Direct Service Delivery

splits.definition: License replicable service (video editing, social management) to partners at fixed margin.
Launch new service by solving avatar-specific problems in person.

kick_members.headline: Offer two-tier workouts (hard tier 1, regressive tier 2) so newbies train alongside OGs without needing separate sessions or complex progressions.
kick_members.reason: Fails topical coherence; about workout design, not licensing or service model. Outlier in cluster domain.

definition: Scale via licensing leverage, not direct service delivery.
rationale: Licensing (replicable, passive) vs. Services (bespoke, labor-intensive) are distinct business model choices. Exercise programming is unrelated tactic.

definition: Door-to-door generates 20+ deals/month consistently; likely higher ROI than paid advertising.
rationale: Tight cluster (0.91 min sim); all paraphrases of same claim. Accept as coherent, load-bearing assertion.

canonical_name: Churn as Growth Ceiling in Agency Models

splits.definition: Selling client acquisition to non-sales-capable founders creates churn; lack of usage activation is the bottleneck.
Annual churn 40-50% in agency/service models masks acquisition inefficiency; fixing churn >growth scaling.
Replace transactional customer acquisition with compounding distributor relationships.

definition: Churn fundamentally constrains growth; must fix before scaling acquisition.
rationale: Cluster mixes three distinct levers: (1) avatar selection mismatch, (2) churn as hidden constraint, (3) distribution model shift. Each requires different intervention.

splits.definition: Lead nurture maximizes show rates between ad impression and sales call; post-interest, pre-purchase.
Build attention/audience pre-product; attention-grabbing tactics create leverage multiplier for future launches.

definition: Nurture bridges awareness (audience) and action (sales) via engaged lead conversion.
rationale: Conflates definitional (what is lead nurture) with tactical (how to grab attention). Split separates funnel stage from execution technique.
